title: Public Event Page Platform
description: A personal full-stack project inspired by Eventbrite, focused on creating a lower-cost event platform for hosts and attendees. The platform is being designed to support account creation, event uploads, event verification, browsing, and ticket purchasing.

title: Simple Centipede
description: A playable Centipede-style game built with Python, Pygame, and CircuitPython. The game combines desktop gameplay with Circuit Playground Express hardware input, allowing users to control movement with accelerometer tilt and receive NeoPixel feedback.

title: Unity Space Fighter Prototype
description: A functional 2D Unity game prototype where the player controls a space fighter to destroy grey debris and organise red, green, and blue particles into scoring patterns. The prototype includes random particle spawning, movement, collision handling, grid storage, scoring, pause/resume, HUD feedback, and win/loss states.
demoStatus: A browser-playable Unity WebGL demo is available from the Live Demo button. Click inside the game window before using the keyboard controls.
howToPlay: Click the Start button to begin the game and start the timer.
Use the arrow keys to move the space fighter around the play area.
Use the space fighter to push red, green, and blue ionised particles sideways into the storage grid.
Destroy grey debris by hitting it from underneath with the space fighter.
Press Spacebar to pause or resume the game.
rules: Particles spawn from the top-centre of the flow area every 2 seconds and fall downward.
The spawner randomly chooses red, green, blue, or grey particles, while preventing the same particle type from spawning twice in a row.
Ionised particles are stored in a 5 by 5 grid and snap into the nearest available column when they settle.
Three matching coloured particles in a vertical line are removed from the grid.
A horizontal red-green-blue sequence is removed from the grid.
When a group is removed, particles above it drop down into the available spaces.
scoring: Destroying one debris particle gives 5 points.
Creating a vertical group of three matching red, green, or blue particles gives 10 points.
Creating a horizontal RGB group gives 15 points.
The player wins when the score reaches 100.
The player loses if the storage grid fills with ungrouped particles or if 5 debris particles enter storage.

title: Personal Portfolio Website
description: A Vue.js portfolio website adapted and customised to present my education, technical skills, project work, achievements, and contact details. The site is deployed through GitHub Pages and updated through Git commits.
